subject: Reformat code according to Java Code Guidelines
commit_message:
Reformat code according to Java Code Guidelines

Align appc-config-adaptor-provider implementation classes with ONAP
and Google Java Style guidelines: K&R Style brackets, 120 column limit,
indentation fixes, method and property reorder according to visibility.

Change-Id: I079e6d9eec5f400db00c5bf41937ca20dd778e72
Issue-ID: APPC-361
Signed-off-by: Michal Kabaj <michal.kabaj@nokia.com>

Patch Set 4:

Patch Set 3:

> Hi Michal.   Changes look good except for one question about the
 > method/property re-ordering.   Is the re-ordering necessary per the
 > java style guidelines or for the readability of the code?   If not,
 > I think the original order should be maintained and reduce the
 > churn in the change logs.


Hello Skip,
I have reverted my changes back to the previous method order, I only left the property order. Let me elaborate why.
The java style guideline does not specifically state how specific content should be arranged inside a class, only that it is ordered by "some logical order" which improves readability and is easy to understand for a new developer. One of the more generally recommended approaches and the one I use is to group methods by their functionality. This way it is easier to extract and split code into smaller classes, easier to maintain and test. Unfortunately this requires writing Junits and performing refactoring on the current code. The method order introduced by my changes was based on the visibility and accessibility (first public static, public, protected, package private, private, etc) which indeed may result in more confusion. That is why I have reverted the changes back to the previous order.
The property ordering by visibility and scope introduced by my changes should be kept. There seemed to be no order consistency between classes and functional grouping is not possible for fields (properties define structure, not behavior). Also this is how popular IDEs arrange the properties by default when formatting the code. (static fields before instance fields). Hope this clarifies a bit :)

Best Regards,
Michal

Patch Set 3:

> For example, in SshJscrfWrapper.java. you moved some methods back
 > and forth. Is there any reason that you want to do that?

Hi Takamune,
I have reverted my changes and restored the method order to the way it was. Please check the full explanation in my reply to Skip :)

Best Regards,
Michal
